hypothesis: If generative models for Covalent Triazine Frameworks are pre-trained on large chemical corpora (ChEMBL/ZINC), fine-tuned on a small CTF-specific dataset, and then optimized with reinforcement learning using expert-designed rewards, then they will produce samples with higher validity, novelty, internal diversity, Fréchet ChemNet Distance, and expert-filter pass rates than models trained without these transfer steps because multi-stage knowledge transfer combines broad chemical priors with task-specific structural constraints to guide exploration.
